# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home)

This file provides detailed guidance for the frontend static site.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON is current (fails if stale)
npm run build           # Alias for build:data

# Local development
npx serve .             # or npx http-server . -o
```

## Data Pipeline (CSV -> JSON)

### Source Files (DO NOT edit JSON directly)
```
golfers/golfers.csv  -->  data/golfers.json
leagues/leagues.csv  -->  data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv      -->  data/tours.json
```

### CSV Schema: Golfers
Columns: `name,location,handicap,longdrive,longputt,bestscore,holeinone,year`

Rules:
- Empty numeric cells -> `null` in JSON
- Location: two-letter lowercase (e.g., `mi` for Michigan)
- UTF-8 encoding, comma-delimited, header row required

### CSV Schema: Leagues/Tours
Columns: `name,location,golfers,year`

### Workflow for CSV Changes
```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files together
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
```

## File Structure

```
lzg.home/
├── index.html, about.html, ...     # Static pages
├── login.html, profile.html        # Auth pages
├── golfers/
│   ├── golfers.csv                 # SOURCE OF TRUTH
│   ├── script.js                   # Page-specific logic
│   └── golfer_stats.html           # Detail page
├── leagues/, tours/                # Same pattern
├── js/
│   ├── auth.js                     # OAuth + session (window.lazyAuth)
│   ├── profile.js                  # Profile CRUD
│   ├── rounds.js                   # Rounds management
│   ├── includes.js                 # Loads header/footer partials
│   ├── table-utils.js              # Sorting/filtering helpers
│   └── [vendor libs]               # jQuery, Bootstrap, etc.
├── data/                           # GENERATED - DO NOT EDIT
├── partials/
│   ├── header.html                 # Shared header
│   └── footer.html                 # Shared footer
└── scripts/
    └── convert-csv.js              # CSV -> JSON converter
```

## Authentication Client (js/auth.js)

Global API client available on all pages:

```javascript
window.lazyAuth = {
  API_BASE,                         // 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'
  request(path, options),           // Fetch wrapper with credentials
  getSession(),                     // Check login status
  getMe(),                          // Get user profile
  updateMe(payload),                // Update profile
  logout(),                         // Clear session
  redirectToGoogleLogin(),          // Start OAuth
};

// Usage
const session = await window.lazyAuth.getSession();
if (session.authenticated) {
  const profile = await window.lazyAuth.getMe();
}
```

## Adding a New Data Source

```bash
# 1. Create CSV
mkdir -p courses
cat > courses/courses.csv << EOF
name,location,holes,par,year
Pebble Beach,ca,18,72,1919
EOF

# 2. Update scripts/convert-csv.js - add to datasets array:
{
  label: 'courses',
  csv: path.resolve(__dirname, '../courses/courses.csv'),
  json: path.resolve(__dirname, '../data/courses.json'),
}

# 3. Generate JSON
npm run build:data

# 4. Create page script
cat > courses/script.js << 'EOF'
(function() {
  fetch('/data/courses.json')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(data => { /* render */ });
})();
EOF

# 5. Create HTML page referencing the script
```

## Debugging

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for `/data/*.json` |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | Run `npm run build:data` |
| 401 on `/v1/me` | Not logged in | Check cookies, re-login |
| CORS errors | Origin not allowed | Add to backend CORS allowlist |

```javascript
// Browser console debugging
await window.lazyAuth.getSession()
await window.lazyAuth.getMe()
```

## Patterns

- **No build pipeline** - Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES6 JS served directly
- **jQuery + Bootstrap 5** - Use consistently
- **IIFE pattern** - Wrap page scripts `(function() { ... })();`
- **Partials loading** - `includes.js` injects header/footer via fetch
- **Data fetching** - Always use `fetch('/data/*.json')` with error handling

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

This file provides detailed guidance for the NestJS API backend.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev               # Watch mode with hot-reload
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run build                   # Compile TypeScript -> dist/
npm run start:prod              # Run compiled app
npm run lint                    # ESLint with auto-fix
npm run format                  # Prettier

# Testing
npm run test                    # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run test:watch              # Watch mode
npm run test:cov                # With coverage
npm run test:e2e                # End-to-end tests

# Database
npx prisma generate             # Regenerate client (REQUIRED after schema changes)
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma migrate deploy       # Production-safe migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (localhost:5555)
npx prisma db push              # Push schema without migration
npx prisma format               # Format schema.prisma

# Seeding
npm run prisma:seed             # Run seed script
npm run seed:courses            # Seed course data

# Production (PM2)
pm2 start ecosystem.config.js
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
pm2 stop lg-api
```

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Detailed Documentation

For in-depth guidance on each project, see:
- **[lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](lzg.home/CLAUDE.md)** - Frontend: CSV data pipeline, auth client, file structure, adding data sources
- **[lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md)** - Backend: API endpoints, Prisma schema, authentication flow, adding endpoints

Read these when working on the respective project.

---

## Repository Structure

**Monorepo** with two projects:
- `lzg.home/` - Static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) hosted on Cloudflare Pages
- `lzg.backend/` - NestJS API (TypeScript) with PostgreSQL/Prisma

## Quick Commands

### Frontend (lzg.home/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion (golfers, leagues, tours)
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON matches CSV (CI check, fails if stale)
```

### Backend (lzg.backend/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev       # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test            # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run lint            # ESLint with auto-fix

# Database
npx prisma generate     # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio       # Database GUI
```

### Production
```bash
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
```

## Architecture Overview

### Data Flow (Frontend)
```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

**Rule:** Always commit both CSV and JSON together. Never edit JSON directly.

### Backend Modules
```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models
- `User` - OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player
- `Player` - Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds
- `Round` - Golf rounds with advanced stats
- `Course` - Course data with API integration
- `RoundLink` - Playing partner linking
- `Role/UserRole` - RBAC
- `AuditLog` - Action tracking

### Authentication
- Google OAuth -> JWT in HttpOnly cookie (`lg_jwt`, 7-day expiry)
- Protected routes: `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`
- User access: `@CurrentUser()` decorator
- Frontend client: `window.lazyAuth` in `js/auth.js`

### Key Endpoints
- `GET /auth/google` - Start OAuth
- `GET /auth/session` - Check login
- `GET /v1/me` - Get profile (auth required)
- `PUT /v1/me` - Update profile (auth required)
- `GET/POST /v1/rounds` - Rounds CRUD (auth required)

## Critical Rules

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never modify CSV without confirmation, always run `build:data` after edits
2. **Run `npx prisma generate`** after any schema.prisma change
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs**
5. **Backend tests must pass** - `npm run test`
6. **Frontend check must pass** - `npm run check:data`

## Environment Variables (Backend)

```
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

- https://lazygolfing.com
- https://www.lazygolfing.com
- https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev

Add `http://localhost:*` for local development.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Detailed Documentation

For in-depth guidance on each project, see:
- **[lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](lzg.home/CLAUDE.md)** - Frontend: CSV data pipeline, auth client, file structure, adding data sources
- **[lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md)** - Backend: API endpoints, Prisma schema, authentication flow, adding endpoints

Read these when working on the respective project.

---

## Repository Structure

**Monorepo** with two projects:
- `lzg.home/` - Static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) hosted on Cloudflare Pages
- `lzg.backend/` - NestJS API (TypeScript) with PostgreSQL/Prisma

## Quick Commands

### Frontend (lzg.home/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion (golfers, leagues, tours)
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON matches CSV (CI check, fails if stale)
```

### Backend (lzg.backend/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev       # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test            # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run lint            # ESLint with auto-fix

# Database
npx prisma generate     # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio       # Database GUI
```

### Production
```bash
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
```

## Architecture Overview

### Data Flow (Frontend)
```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

**Rule:** Always commit both CSV and JSON together. Never edit JSON directly.

### Backend Modules
```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models
- `User` - OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player
- `Player` - Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds
- `Round` - Golf rounds with advanced stats
- `Course` - Course data with API integration
- `RoundLink` - Playing partner linking
- `Role/UserRole` - RBAC
- `AuditLog` - Action tracking

### Authentication
- Google OAuth -> JWT in HttpOnly cookie (`lg_jwt`, 7-day expiry)
- Protected routes: `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`
- User access: `@CurrentUser()` decorator
- Frontend client: `window.lazyAuth` in `js/auth.js`

### Key Endpoints
- `GET /auth/google` - Start OAuth
- `GET /auth/session` - Check login
- `GET /v1/me` - Get profile (auth required)
- `PUT /v1/me` - Update profile (auth required)
- `GET/POST /v1/rounds` - Rounds CRUD (auth required)

## Critical Rules

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never modify CSV without confirmation, always run `build:data` after edits
2. **Run `npx prisma generate`** after any schema.prisma change
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs**
5. **Backend tests must pass** - `npm run test`
6. **Frontend check must pass** - `npm run check:data`

## Environment Variables (Backend)

```
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

- https://lazygolfing.com
- https://www.lazygolfing.com
- https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev

Add `http://localhost:*` for local development.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository Structure

**Monorepo** with two projects:
- `lzg.home/` - Static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) hosted on Cloudflare Pages
- `lzg.backend/` - NestJS API (TypeScript) with PostgreSQL/Prisma

## Commands

### Frontend (lzg.home/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion (golfers, leagues, tours)
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON matches CSV (CI check, fails if stale)
npm run build           # Runs build:data
```

### Backend (lzg.backend/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev       # Development server with hot-reload
npm run start:debug     # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run build           # Compile TypeScript -> dist/
npm run lint            # ESLint with auto-fix
npm run format          # Prettier

# Testing
npm run test            # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run test:watch      # Watch mode
npm run test:cov        # With coverage
npm run test:e2e        # End-to-end tests

# Database
npx prisma generate     # Regenerate client after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio       # Database GUI (localhost:5555)
npx prisma db push      # Push schema without migration
```

### Production (Backend)
```bash
pm2 start ecosystem.config.js
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
```

## Architecture

### Data Flow
```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

**Rule:** Always commit both CSV and JSON together. Never edit JSON directly.

### Backend Modules
```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models (Prisma)
- `User` - OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player
- `Player` - Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds
- `Round` - Golf rounds with advanced stats (GIR%, FIR%, putts, scoring breakdown)
- `Course` - Course data with API integration
- `RoundLink` - Playing partner linking between rounds
- `Role/UserRole` - RBAC
- `AuditLog` - Action tracking

### Authentication
- Google OAuth -> JWT in HttpOnly cookie (`lg_jwt`, 7-day expiry)
- Protected routes use `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`
- `@CurrentUser()` decorator extracts user from request
- Frontend: `window.lazyAuth` global API client in `js/auth.js`

### Key API Endpoints
- `GET /auth/google` - Start OAuth flow
- `GET /auth/session` - Check login status
- `GET /auth/logout` - Clear session
- `GET /v1/me` - Get profile (requires auth)
- `PUT /v1/me` - Update profile (requires auth)
- `GET /v1/rounds` - List rounds (requires auth)
- `POST /v1/rounds` - Create round (requires auth)

## Critical Rules

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never modify CSV without confirmation, always run `build:data` after edits
2. **Run `npx prisma generate`** after any schema.prisma change
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs**
5. **Backend tests must pass before completing tasks** - `npm run test`
6. **Frontend check must pass** - `npm run check:data`

## Environment Variables (Backend)
```
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## CORS Origins (src/main.ts)
- https://lazygolfing.com
- https://www.lazygolfing.com
- https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev

Add `http://localhost:*` for local development.

## Frontend Patterns

- **No build pipeline** - Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES6 JS served directly
- **jQuery + Bootstrap 5** - Consistent throughout
- **Shared partials** - `partials/header.html`, `partials/footer.html` loaded by `js/includes.js`
- **Data loading** - `fetch('/data/*.json')` with graceful error handling
- **Auth client** - `window.lazyAuth.getSession()`, `.getMe()`, `.updateMe()`, `.request()`

## Debugging

```bash
# Backend logs
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100

# Test API
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=..." https://api.lazygolfing.com/v1/me

# Check JSON freshness
npm run check:data
```

- Local frontend development would either hit production API or need CORS configured for the developer's machine

I should ask for clarification on their workflow:

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Detailed Documentation

For in-depth guidance on each project, see:
- **[lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](lzg.home/CLAUDE.md)** - Frontend: CSV data pipeline, auth client, file structure, adding data sources
- **[lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md)** - Backend: API endpoints, Prisma schema, authentication flow, adding endpoints

Read these when working on the respective project.

---

## Repository Structure

**Monorepo** with two projects:
- `lzg.home/` - Static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS) hosted on Cloudflare Pages
- `lzg.backend/` - NestJS API (TypeScript) with PostgreSQL/Prisma

## Quick Commands

### Frontend (lzg.home/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion (golfers, leagues, tours)
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON matches CSV (CI check, fails if stale)
```

### Backend (lzg.backend/)
```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev       # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test            # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run lint            # ESLint with auto-fix

# Database
npx prisma generate     # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio       # Database GUI
```

### Production
```bash
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
```

## Architecture Overview

### Data Flow (Frontend)
```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

**Rule:** Always commit both CSV and JSON together. Never edit JSON directly.

### Backend Modules
```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models
- `User` - OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player
- `Player` - Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds
- `Round` - Golf rounds with advanced stats
- `Course` - Course data with API integration
- `RoundLink` - Playing partner linking
- `Role/UserRole` - RBAC
- `AuditLog` - Action tracking

### Authentication
- Google OAuth -> JWT in HttpOnly cookie (`lg_jwt`, 7-day expiry)
- Protected routes: `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`
- User access: `@CurrentUser()` decorator
- Frontend client: `window.lazyAuth` in `js/auth.js`

### Key Endpoints
- `GET /auth/google` - Start OAuth
- `GET /auth/session` - Check login
- `GET /v1/me` - Get profile (auth required)
- `PUT /v1/me` - Update profile (auth required)
- `GET/POST /v1/rounds` - Rounds CRUD (auth required)

## Critical Rules

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never modify CSV without confirmation, always run `build:data` after edits
2. **Run `npx prisma generate`** after any schema.prisma change
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs**
5. **Backend tests must pass** - `npm run test`
6. **Frontend check must pass** - `npm run check:data`

## Environment Variables (Backend)

```
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

- https://lazygolfing.com
- https://www.lazygolfing.com
- https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev

Add `http://localhost:*` for local development.

npm run start:dev       # Development server with hot-reload

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

This file provides detailed guidance for the NestJS API backend.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev               # Watch mode with hot-reload
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run build                   # Compile TypeScript -> dist/
npm run start:prod              # Run compiled app
npm run lint                    # ESLint with auto-fix
npm run format                  # Prettier

# Testing
npm run test                    # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run test:watch              # Watch mode
npm run test:cov                # With coverage
npm run test:e2e                # End-to-end tests

# Production (PM2)

pm2 stop lg-api
```

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

This file provides detailed guidance for the NestJS API backend.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run start:dev               # Watch mode with hot-reload
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run build                   # Compile TypeScript -> dist/
npm run start:prod              # Run compiled app
npm run lint                    # ESLint with auto-fix
npm run format                  # Prettier

# Testing
npm run test                    # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run test:watch              # Watch mode
npm run test:cov                # With coverage
npm run test:e2e                # End-to-end tests

# Database
npx prisma generate             # Regenerate client (REQUIRED after schema changes)
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma migrate deploy       # Production-safe migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (localhost:5555)
npx prisma db push              # Push schema without migration
npx prisma format               # Format schema.prisma

# Seeding
npm run prisma:seed             # Run seed script
npm run seed:courses            # Seed course data

# Production (PM2)
pm2 start ecosystem.config.js
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
pm2 stop lg-api
```

## Environment Variables

Required in `.env`:
```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
DIRECT_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module
├── app.controller.ts           # Health check (GET /)
│
├── auth/                       # Authentication
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Admin-only guard
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF protection
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
│
├── profile/                    # User profile
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
│
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts
│
├── courses/                    # Course management
│   ├── courses.controller.ts
│   ├── courses.service.ts
│   └── dto/
│
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   ├── prisma.module.ts
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
│
├── audit-log/                  # Action logging
│   └── audit-log.service.ts
│
└── config/
    └── env.validation.ts       # Environment validation
```

## Database Schema (prisma/schema.prisma)

### Models & Relationships
```
User (OAuth identity)
  ├── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
  └── N:N Role via UserRole

Player
  └── 1:N Round

Round
  ├── N:1 Player
  ├── N:1 Course (optional)
  └── N:N RoundLink (playing partners)

Course
  └── 1:N Round

AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields
- `User`: email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt
- `Player`: firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap
- `Round`: strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys, etc.
- `Course`: name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId, apiData (JSON)

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | Check login status |
| GET | `/auth/logout` | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |

### Rounds (Requires JWT)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |

### Courses
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses` | List/search courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | Get course details |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | Create course (admin) |

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend -> GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google -> /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() -> User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config (main.ts)
```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes
```typescript
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload
}
```

## Adding a New Endpoint

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method (src/[module]/[module].service.ts)
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route (src/[module]/[module].controller.ts)
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests
npm run test
```

## Updating Prisma Schema

```bash
# 1. Edit prisma/schema.prisma

# 2. Format
npx prisma format

# 3. Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_thing_field

# 4. Regenerate client
npx prisma generate

# 5. Update DTOs and services to use new field

# 6. Test
npm run test
```

## CORS Configuration (src/main.ts)

```typescript
origin: [
  'https://lazygolfing.com',
  'https://www.lazygolfing.com',
  'https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev',
  // Add for local dev:
  // 'http://localhost:8080',
]
```

## Debugging

```bash
# Check logs
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100

# Test endpoints
curl http://localhost:3000/
curl http://localhost:3000/auth/session
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=..." http://localhost:3000/v1/me

# Enable Prisma query logging (schema.prisma)
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  log      = ["query", "info", "warn", "error"]
}
```

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| Migration failed | DB not running | Check PostgreSQL status |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Check cookie, re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to main.ts allowlist |
| 500 Internal Server Error | Uncaught exception | Check PM2 logs |

## Validation Rules (DTOs)

```typescript
// Profile (update-profile.dto.ts)
firstName: 1-50 chars
lastName: 1-50 chars
ghinNumber: 7 digits (string)
handicap: 0-54 (float)

// Round (create-round.dto.ts)
courseName: 1-100 chars
strokes: positive integer
date: ISO 8601 datetime (optional)
notes: max 500 chars (optional)
```

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

This file provides detailed guidance for the NestJS API backend.

## Development Setup

- **Code editing**: Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime**: Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API**: `api.lazygolfing.com`

Code is edited locally and synced/deployed to the VM where it runs.

## Commands (Run on VM - 192.168.1.40)

```bash
# SSH to VM first, then run these commands

npm ci
npm run start:dev               # Watch mode with hot-reload
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run build                   # Compile TypeScript -> dist/
npm run start:prod              # Run compiled app
npm run lint                    # ESLint with auto-fix
npm run format                  # Prettier

# Testing
npm run test                    # Unit tests (*.spec.ts)
npm run test:watch              # Watch mode
npm run test:cov                # With coverage
npm run test:e2e                # End-to-end tests

# Database
npx prisma generate             # Regenerate client (REQUIRED after schema changes)
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma migrate deploy       # Production-safe migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (access at 192.168.1.40:5555)
npx prisma db push              # Push schema without migration
npx prisma format               # Format schema.prisma

# Seeding
npm run prisma:seed             # Run seed script
npm run seed:courses            # Seed course data

# Production (PM2)
pm2 start ecosystem.config.js
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100
pm2 restart lg-api
pm2 stop lg-api
```

## Environment Variables

Required in `.env`:
```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
DIRECT_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module
├── app.controller.ts           # Health check (GET /)
│
├── auth/                       # Authentication
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Admin-only guard
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF protection
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
│
├── profile/                    # User profile
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
│
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts
│
├── courses/                    # Course management
│   ├── courses.controller.ts
│   ├── courses.service.ts
│   └── dto/
│
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   ├── prisma.module.ts
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
│
├── audit-log/                  # Action logging
│   └── audit-log.service.ts
│
└── config/
    └── env.validation.ts       # Environment validation
```

## Database Schema (prisma/schema.prisma)

### Models & Relationships
```
User (OAuth identity)
  ├── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
  └── N:N Role via UserRole

Player
  └── 1:N Round

Round
  ├── N:1 Player
  ├── N:1 Course (optional)
  └── N:N RoundLink (playing partners)

Course
  └── 1:N Round

AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields
- `User`: email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt
- `Player`: firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap
- `Round`: strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys, etc.
- `Course`: name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId, apiData (JSON)

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | Check login status |
| GET | `/auth/logout` | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |

### Rounds (Requires JWT)
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |

### Courses
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses` | List/search courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | Get course details |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | Create course (admin) |

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend -> GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google -> /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() -> User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config (main.ts)
```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes
```typescript
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload
}
```

## Adding a New Endpoint

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method (src/[module]/[module].service.ts)
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route (src/[module]/[module].controller.ts)
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests
npm run test
```

## Updating Prisma Schema

```bash
# 1. Edit prisma/schema.prisma

# 2. Format
npx prisma format

# 3. Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_thing_field

# 4. Regenerate client
npx prisma generate

# 5. Update DTOs and services to use new field

# 6. Test
npm run test
```

## CORS Configuration (src/main.ts)

```typescript
origin: [
  'https://lazygolfing.com',
  'https://www.lazygolfing.com',
  'https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev',
  // Add for local dev:
  // 'http://localhost:8080',
]
```

## Debugging

```bash
# Check logs
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100

# Test endpoints
curl http://localhost:3000/
curl http://localhost:3000/auth/session
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=..." http://localhost:3000/v1/me

# Enable Prisma query logging (schema.prisma)
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  log      = ["query", "info", "warn", "error"]
}
```

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| Migration failed | DB not running | Check PostgreSQL status |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Check cookie, re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to main.ts allowlist |
| 500 Internal Server Error | Uncaught exception | Check PM2 logs |

## Validation Rules (DTOs)

```typescript
// Profile (update-profile.dto.ts)
firstName: 1-50 chars
lastName: 1-50 chars
ghinNumber: 7 digits (string)
handicap: 0-54 (float)

// Round (create-round.dto.ts)
courseName: 1-100 chars
strokes: positive integer
date: ISO 8601 datetime (optional)
notes: max 500 chars (optional)
```

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home)

This file provides detailed guidance for the frontend static site.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON is current (fails if stale)
npm run build           # Alias for build:data

# Local development
npx serve .             # or npx http-server . -o
```

## Data Pipeline (CSV -> JSON)

### Source Files (DO NOT edit JSON directly)
```
golfers/golfers.csv  -->  data/golfers.json
leagues/leagues.csv  -->  data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv      -->  data/tours.json
```

### CSV Schema: Golfers
Columns: `name,location,handicap,longdrive,longputt,bestscore,holeinone,year`

Rules:
- Empty numeric cells -> `null` in JSON
- Location: two-letter lowercase (e.g., `mi` for Michigan)
- UTF-8 encoding, comma-delimited, header row required

### CSV Schema: Leagues/Tours
Columns: `name,location,golfers,year`

### Workflow for CSV Changes
```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files together
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
```

## File Structure

```
lzg.home/
├── index.html, about.html, ...     # Static pages
├── login.html, profile.html        # Auth pages
├── golfers/
│   ├── golfers.csv                 # SOURCE OF TRUTH
│   ├── script.js                   # Page-specific logic
│   └── golfer_stats.html           # Detail page
├── leagues/, tours/                # Same pattern
├── js/
│   ├── auth.js                     # OAuth + session (window.lazyAuth)
│   ├── profile.js                  # Profile CRUD
│   ├── rounds.js                   # Rounds management
│   ├── includes.js                 # Loads header/footer partials
│   ├── table-utils.js              # Sorting/filtering helpers
│   └── [vendor libs]               # jQuery, Bootstrap, etc.
├── data/                           # GENERATED - DO NOT EDIT
├── partials/
│   ├── header.html                 # Shared header
│   └── footer.html                 # Shared footer
└── scripts/
    └── convert-csv.js              # CSV -> JSON converter
```

## Authentication Client (js/auth.js)

Global API client available on all pages:

```javascript
window.lazyAuth = {
  API_BASE,                         // 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'
  request(path, options),           // Fetch wrapper with credentials
  getSession(),                     // Check login status
  getMe(),                          // Get user profile
  updateMe(payload),                // Update profile
  logout(),                         // Clear session
  redirectToGoogleLogin(),          // Start OAuth
};

// Usage
const session = await window.lazyAuth.getSession();
if (session.authenticated) {
  const profile = await window.lazyAuth.getMe();
}
```

## Adding a New Data Source

```bash
# 1. Create CSV
mkdir -p courses
cat > courses/courses.csv << EOF
name,location,holes,par,year
Pebble Beach,ca,18,72,1919
EOF

# 2. Update scripts/convert-csv.js - add to datasets array:
{
  label: 'courses',
  csv: path.resolve(__dirname, '../courses/courses.csv'),
  json: path.resolve(__dirname, '../data/courses.json'),
}

# 3. Generate JSON
npm run build:data

# 4. Create page script
cat > courses/script.js << 'EOF'
(function() {
  fetch('/data/courses.json')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(data => { /* render */ });
})();
EOF

# 5. Create HTML page referencing the script
```

## Debugging

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for `/data/*.json` |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | Run `npm run build:data` |
| 401 on `/v1/me` | Not logged in | Check cookies, re-login |
| CORS errors | Origin not allowed | Add to backend CORS allowlist |

```javascript
// Browser console debugging
await window.lazyAuth.getSession()
await window.lazyAuth.getMe()
```

## Patterns

- **No build pipeline** - Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES6 JS served directly
- **jQuery + Bootstrap 5** - Use consistently
- **IIFE pattern** - Wrap page scripts `(function() { ... })();`
- **Partials loading** - `includes.js` injects header/footer via fetch
- **Data fetching** - Always use `fetch('/data/*.json')` with error handling

```bash
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files together
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
```

## File Structure

```
lzg.home/
├── index.html, about.html, ...     # Static pages
├── login.html, profile.html        # Auth pages
├── golfers/
│   ├── golfers.csv                 # SOURCE OF TRUTH
│   ├── script.js                   # Page-specific logic
│   └── golfer_stats.html           # Detail page
├── leagues/, tours/                # Same pattern
├── js/
│   ├── auth.js                     # OAuth + session (window.lazyAuth)
│   ├── profile.js                  # Profile CRUD
│   ├── rounds.js                   # Rounds management
│   ├── includes.js                 # Loads header/footer partials
│   ├── table-utils.js              # Sorting/filtering helpers
│   └── [vendor libs]               # jQuery, Bootstrap, etc.
├── data/                           # GENERATED - DO NOT EDIT
├── partials/
│   ├── header.html                 # Shared header
│   └── footer.html                 # Shared footer
└── scripts/
    └── convert-csv.js              # CSV -> JSON converter
```

## Authentication Client (js/auth.js)

Global API client available on all pages:

```javascript
window.lazyAuth = {
  API_BASE,                         // 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'
  request(path, options),           // Fetch wrapper with credentials
  getSession(),                     // Check login status
  getMe(),                          // Get user profile
  updateMe(payload),                // Update profile
  logout(),                         // Clear session
  redirectToGoogleLogin(),          // Start OAuth
};

// Usage
const session = await window.lazyAuth.getSession();
if (session.authenticated) {
  const profile = await window.lazyAuth.getMe();
}
```

## Adding a New Data Source

```bash
# 1. Create CSV
mkdir -p courses
cat > courses/courses.csv << EOF
name,location,holes,par,year
Pebble Beach,ca,18,72,1919
EOF

# 2. Update scripts/convert-csv.js - add to datasets array:
{
  label: 'courses',
  csv: path.resolve(__dirname, '../courses/courses.csv'),
  json: path.resolve(__dirname, '../data/courses.json'),
}

# 3. Generate JSON
npm run build:data

# 4. Create page script
cat > courses/script.js << 'EOF'
(function() {
  fetch('/data/courses.json')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(data => { /* render */ });
})();
EOF

# 5. Create HTML page referencing the script
```

## Debugging

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for `/data/*.json` |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | Run `npm run build:data` |
| 401 on `/v1/me` | Not logged in | Check cookies, re-login |
| CORS errors | Origin not allowed | Add to backend CORS allowlist |

```javascript
// Browser console debugging
await window.lazyAuth.getSession()
await window.lazyAuth.getMe()
```

## Patterns

- **No build pipeline** - Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES6 JS served directly
- **jQuery + Bootstrap 5** - Use consistently
- **IIFE pattern** - Wrap page scripts `(function() { ... })();`
- **Partials loading** - `includes.js` injects header/footer via fetch
- **Data fetching** - Always use `fetch('/data/*.json')` with error handling

+To test against the dev API, change `API_BASE` in `js/auth.js` or `js/config.js` to `http://192.168.1.40:3000`.

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home)

This file provides detailed guidance for the frontend static site.

## Development Setup

- **Code editing & serving**: This Windows machine
- **Backend API (dev)**: Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000`
- **Backend API (prod)**: `api.lazygolfing.com`

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run build:data      # CSV -> JSON conversion
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON is current (fails if stale)
npm run build           # Alias for build:data

# Local development
npx serve .             # or npx http-server . -o
```

## Data Pipeline (CSV -> JSON)

### Source Files (DO NOT edit JSON directly)
```
golfers/golfers.csv  -->  data/golfers.json
leagues/leagues.csv  -->  data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv      -->  data/tours.json
```

### CSV Schema: Golfers
Columns: `name,location,handicap,longdrive,longputt,bestscore,holeinone,year`

Rules:
- Empty numeric cells -> `null` in JSON
- Location: two-letter lowercase (e.g., `mi` for Michigan)
- UTF-8 encoding, comma-delimited, header row required

### CSV Schema: Leagues/Tours
Columns: `name,location,golfers,year`

### Workflow for CSV Changes
```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files together
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
```

## File Structure

```
lzg.home/
├── index.html, about.html, ...     # Static pages
├── login.html, profile.html        # Auth pages
├── golfers/
│   ├── golfers.csv                 # SOURCE OF TRUTH
│   ├── script.js                   # Page-specific logic
│   └── golfer_stats.html           # Detail page
├── leagues/, tours/                # Same pattern
├── js/
│   ├── auth.js                     # OAuth + session (window.lazyAuth)
│   ├── profile.js                  # Profile CRUD
│   ├── rounds.js                   # Rounds management
│   ├── includes.js                 # Loads header/footer partials
│   ├── table-utils.js              # Sorting/filtering helpers
│   └── [vendor libs]               # jQuery, Bootstrap, etc.
├── data/                           # GENERATED - DO NOT EDIT
├── partials/
│   ├── header.html                 # Shared header
│   └── footer.html                 # Shared footer
└── scripts/
    └── convert-csv.js              # CSV -> JSON converter
```

## Authentication Client (js/auth.js)

Global API client available on all pages:

```javascript
window.lazyAuth = {
  API_BASE,                         // Production: 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'
                                    // Dev (LAN): 'http://192.168.1.40:3000'
  request(path, options),           // Fetch wrapper with credentials
  getSession(),                     // Check login status
  getMe(),                          // Get user profile
  updateMe(payload),                // Update profile
  logout(),                         // Clear session
  redirectToGoogleLogin(),          // Start OAuth
};

// Usage
const session = await window.lazyAuth.getSession();
if (session.authenticated) {
  const profile = await window.lazyAuth.getMe();
}
```

To test against the dev API, change `API_BASE` in `js/auth.js` or `js/config.js` to `http://192.168.1.40:3000`.

## Adding a New Data Source

```bash
# 1. Create CSV
mkdir -p courses
cat > courses/courses.csv << EOF
name,location,holes,par,year
Pebble Beach,ca,18,72,1919
EOF

# 2. Update scripts/convert-csv.js - add to datasets array:
{
  label: 'courses',
  csv: path.resolve(__dirname, '../courses/courses.csv'),
  json: path.resolve(__dirname, '../data/courses.json'),
}

# 3. Generate JSON
npm run build:data

# 4. Create page script
cat > courses/script.js << 'EOF'
(function() {
  fetch('/data/courses.json')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(data => { /* render */ });
})();
EOF

# 5. Create HTML page referencing the script
```

## Debugging

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for `/data/*.json` |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | Run `npm run build:data` |
| 401 on `/v1/me` | Not logged in | Check cookies, re-login |
| CORS errors | Origin not allowed | Add to backend CORS allowlist |

```javascript
// Browser console debugging
await window.lazyAuth.getSession()
await window.lazyAuth.getMe()
```

## Patterns

- **No build pipeline** - Vanilla HTML/CSS/ES6 JS served directly
- **jQuery + Bootstrap 5** - Use consistently
- **IIFE pattern** - Wrap page scripts `(function() { ... })();`
- **Partials loading** - `includes.js` injects header/footer via fetch
- **Data fetching** - Always use `fetch('/data/*.json')` with error handling
